facts.value: brown, short shag grown a little past where a cut would be
Boxy; heavy intramural sport keeps it from going to fat; body is functional, not for being looked at
wears none — eschewed deliberately, not from lack
old rust-spotted SUV with squeaking suspension; kept because it works and hauls equipment
shorts in warm weather, pants in cold; no skirts
Cassie's side of the room may be neater than Vee's; the contrast is part of the friendship

value.claim: Cassie was in a sorority / inside that social world
value.correct_statement: Cassie is unaffiliated — never in a sorority, never inside that social world (same as Vee)

value.claim: Cassie knows she is Cassandra / the device is named or explained on-page
value.correct_statement: She does not know she is Cassandra; the device is never named, explained, or commented on by any character including herself

facts.value: reads as asexual — AUTHOR-KNOWLEDGE ONLY; never surfaces, never named, never any character's observation; shapes rendering (un-watched body, warmth that wants nothing) but is never content
Plain
keeps dorm room window cracked two inches due to preference for cold; building runs heat too high
brown, deliberate — does not twin Randi's black-on-black, does not flatter
Softball in spring, basketball in winter; joins any rec team given the chance
The un-coded third model; opts out of the appearance economy entirely (a 'fourth fact,' not a judgment on the others)
Functional, indifferent-to-venue uniform: ball cap pulled low, headphones, shorts and yesterday's shirt
A marker the reader tracks — voices unease early, gets dismissed, later vindicated
Wears headphones at volume low enough that she can laugh at things said two tables over
Registers structure, not character — perceives arrangement, cost, wrongness-of-fit, and her friend receding
Reports perceptions as flat observations or dry jokes, never as articulated accusations
Pre-nursing student
If the reader starts growing curious about Cassie for her own sake, that is the over-feed signal — pull back
Can see the readout but not the mechanism
Registers structure/operation rather than character; reads the operation instead of receiving the beam
Prefers a sports bar; would choose to be there
keeps dorm room window cracked two inches regardless of cold; prefers the chill to building's overheated air
Her plainness is the unstated source of her clear sight; having no stake in the appearance economy lets her read Randi for operation rather than being caught in the beam
Standard double dorm room shared with Vee, featuring bunk beds, two desks, and a bathroom shared through a connecting door with the adjacent room
The Cassandra device: sees clearly and is not believed; never named, explained, or commented on by any character including herself; she does not know she is Cassandra
Heavily involved in intramural sport; reliably competing/away during key narrative moments
Right within the available frame; Vee is right on hers; the reader holds both
Sees Randi clearly, because she has no investment in Randi being what she presents as
best friend and roommate for two years; closeness rated 3-4; anchors genuine friendship (warmth without agenda); instant mutual enmity with Randi requires no explanation
unmanaged — not ugly, not slovenly, just not managing how she lands
the body is used, not watched; unmanaged
Rendered almost entirely as effect-on-Vee and she-was-right; no standalone interiority
feet lifted off floor when seated; legs too short to reach

Cassie's plainness places her outside both the appearance economy and the sexual economy, so she has no stake in Randi's allure. With no skin in the game, she reads Randi for operation and intent rather than being caught in Randi's beam like others are.
Has none (author-knowledge / unstated)
Thesis-delivery vehicle at the very end ('the best sex of your life...where do I sign up?')
feet do not reach ground when seated in chair; characteristically sits with feet pulled up off floor
Outside the appearance economy; no investment in being looked at
anchors what genuine friendship looks like — warmth without agenda; serves as control case against which the Randi/Vee orchestrated friendship is read
Anchors what genuine friendship looks like—warmth without agenda; serves as control case against Randi/Vee dynamic corrupted by agenda
Anchors what genuine friendship looks like — warmth without agenda; serves as control case against which the Randi/Vee 'friendship' is read
Stands outside all of Randi's beams; the one who sees the mechanism while everyone else is inside one of them

behavioral_states.trigger: moments requiring warmth, encouragement, or celebration directed at others
behavioral_states.response: offers genuine support and happiness but delivers it with dry skepticism and deadpan practicality, undercutting sentiment with observable doubt or wry observation
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie's expressions of support sound warm but are consistently punctured by a practical or skeptical edge; her happiness for others registers as authentic but never unguarded

behavioral_states.trigger: conversation partner wants to change the subject
behavioral_states.response: lets the pivot happen without resistance or comment
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie was good at that — demonstrates established competence and habitual comfort with subject shifts

behavioral_states.trigger: Cassie has received and accepted information in conversation; interaction is being concluded
behavioral_states.response: Cassie produces a brief, quiet 'mm' sound — a small nonverbal acknowledgment — and redirects her attention away from the conversation
behavioral_states.observable: A distinctive, quiet 'mm' vocalizer used as a closing punctuation before Cassie turns her focus elsewhere

behavioral_states.trigger: moment of warmth, positive emotion, or enthusiasm
behavioral_states.response: follows warmth with a dry, skeptical check; gladness has a flat dry floor under it
behavioral_states.observable: reader observes Cassie expressing positive emotion immediately undercut by skeptical or dry commentary

behavioral_states.trigger: sitting in a chair
behavioral_states.response: feet up off the floor, never reaching it
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie's feet do not touch the floor when she is seated in a chair

behavioral_states.trigger: moments of connection or gladness
behavioral_states.response: displays warmth that maintains a flat, dry, understated emotional floor; gladness tempered by restraint
behavioral_states.observable: reader perceives Cassie's warmth as genuine but dry; she tips her head in moments of connection without theatrical display; the gladness is there but grounded, never effusive

behavioral_states.trigger: about to deliver a skeptical or dry remark during conversation
behavioral_states.response: tilts head as a nonverbal tell
behavioral_states.observable: head tilt preceding skeptical or dry verbal remarks

behavioral_states.trigger: friend shares good news or seeks genuine engagement
behavioral_states.response: pulls out of desk chair, sits on edge of bed with sock feet up and arms around knees, leans in close
behavioral_states.observable: shifts from chair to bed edge, draws knees to chest, closes physical distance, aligns with existing 'goes very still / pulls knees to chest when truly listening' but specifies the full postural configuration and the social trigger

behavioral_states.trigger: when paying close attention to someone
behavioral_states.response: goes very still; physical posture tightens (pulls knees to chest); presence becomes palpable to observer as concentrated focus
behavioral_states.observable: Vee recognizes and describes the stillness as feeling like a hand on her shoulder — a tactile metaphor for the weight of Cassie's undivided attention

behavioral_states.trigger: entering classroom and settling into seat
behavioral_states.response: unpacks granola bar, retrieves wrong textbook, then retrieves correct textbook
behavioral_states.observable: sequence of items removed from bag: granola bar, incorrect textbook, correct textbook

behavioral_states.trigger: Vee speaks; Cassie's attention shifts to her friend
behavioral_states.response: Cassie says 'Okay' softly, in a waiting posture—holding space without intrusion
behavioral_states.observable: Reader hears 'Okay,' Cassie said, soft, waiting. when Cassie is truly receiving what Vee offers; contrasts with her dry jokes and flat observations in other modes

behavioral_states.trigger: When Cassie is listening thoughtfully or offering measured approval
behavioral_states.response: Makes a small 'mm' sound as a habitual closing gesture
behavioral_states.observable: The soft 'mm' vocalization serves as Cassie's characteristic signal of quiet, considered acknowledgment—economical, warm, and consistent with her communication style of flat observations and dry humor rather than elaborate verbal response

behavioral_states.trigger: dormitory occupancy with building heat management
behavioral_states.response: keeps dorm window cracked two inches at all times; prefers cold air to building's excessive heat
behavioral_states.observable: window position maintained at two-inch crack; Cassie's stated or demonstrated preference for cooler temperature over ambient dorm conditions

behavioral_states.trigger: moment of warmth, genuine air, or conversational natural ending between Cassie and Vee
behavioral_states.response: produces a small 'mm' sound — understated verbal tell functioning as her version of a blessing; signals warm, quiet approval or conversational closure
behavioral_states.observable: audible small 'mm' vocalization; brief, subtle, consistent with her low-volume headphone habit and flat expression style; reader recognizes it as affectionate acceptance rather than dismissal

behavioral_states.trigger: cold weather in dorm room
behavioral_states.response: keeps window cracked; maintains cold air over heated excess
behavioral_states.observable: window remains partially open during winter months despite ambient temperature

behavioral_states.trigger: moments of genuine warmth or positive emotion
behavioral_states.response: instinctively undercuts with dry joke or skeptical observation, flattening the affective register
behavioral_states.observable: reader perceives authentic warmth immediately followed by self-deflating humor that prevents sentiment from landing unambiguously

behavioral_states.trigger: receives or encounters good news
behavioral_states.response: exhibits flat affect with skeptical tipping of head; gladness has dry skeptical floor underneath
behavioral_states.observable: head tilt accompanied by flat vocal tone; skepticism registers before warmth

behavioral_states.trigger: Vee walks into the room
behavioral_states.response: Cassie puts down her highlighter and redirects full attention to Vee
behavioral_states.observable: The highlighter is uncapped and set aside; Cassie looks at Vee over her reading material

behavioral_states.trigger: in moments of connection or gladness with Vee
behavioral_states.response: her warmth contains a flat, dry, grounded quality beneath it; gladness paired with skepticism
behavioral_states.observable: the reader perceives warmth in Cassie that never loses its dry floor; she is glad but the gladness is never uncomplicated or naive

behavioral_states.trigger: good news arrives or is shared with her
behavioral_states.response: whole face opens with gladness; readable, unguarded joy
behavioral_states.observable: face visibly brightens; expression becomes open and warm; joy registers fully across features

behavioral_states.trigger: sitting with Vee, moments of attention or emotional engagement
behavioral_states.response: positions herself on edge of Vee's bed with sock feet up and arms around knees
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie sits with knees drawn up to chest, arms wrapped around them, feet bare or in socks, typically on the edge of Vee's bed during close conversation

behavioral_states.trigger: witnesses movement or social interaction in proximity
behavioral_states.response: eyes track the detail (movement, positioning, expression shift), registers it without facial reaction; reports internally or later as flat observation
behavioral_states.observable: Eyes flick to track detail (lap-region under table), return to face, expression remains unchanged — demonstrates simultaneous perception and emotional non-engagement

behavioral_states.trigger: When Vee shares or Cassie acknowledges understanding / agreement
behavioral_states.response: Says 'Okay,' in a soft voice, often with a quality of waiting or openness
behavioral_states.observable: The word 'Okay' spoken softly by Cassie; registers as genuine attention and acceptance rather than dismissal; contrasts with her typically dry, flat expression style

behavioral_states.trigger: sitting anywhere (bed, chair, etc.)
behavioral_states.response: places sock feet up and pulls knees to chest or similar tucked posture
behavioral_states.observable: sock feet elevated, arms around knees, compact seated position maintained regardless of venue

behavioral_states.trigger: invited to group activity or social event
behavioral_states.response: deflects with brief excuse ('I've got a thing'); opts out without elaboration or visible reluctance
behavioral_states.observable: When Cassie is invited to social activities, she responds with a short, matter-of-fact excuse and disengages without drama or explanation

behavioral_states.trigger: attending class
behavioral_states.response: unpacks granola bar and initially retrieves wrong textbook, then retrieves correct textbook
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie arrives to class with snack and multiple textbooks, demonstrates self-correction in material organization

behavioral_states.trigger: when seated, especially when at desk or in dorm
behavioral_states.response: folds into chair with feet up off the floor (feet do not reach when so positioned)
behavioral_states.observable: went back to the chair and folded herself into it, feet up off the floor where they never reached

behavioral_states.trigger: moments of connection or positive feeling regarding someone she cares about
behavioral_states.response: expresses warmth with a characteristic flatness and dryness underneath; gladness tempered by restraint; head-tipping or similar understated physical acknowledgment rather than effusive expression
behavioral_states.observable: when Cassie experiences gladness (e.g., Vee confiding in her), her emotional warmth surfaces but is always grounded in a flat, dry quality — she shows care through restraint and understated gesture rather than overt affect

behavioral_states.trigger: when listening to or receiving information from others, particularly Vee
behavioral_states.response: says 'Okay' softly, with a quality of waiting—present, receiving, non-performative
behavioral_states.observable: recurring soft utterance of 'Okay' paired with posture of attention (stilled, knees-to-chest listening position)

behavioral_states.trigger: Cassie experiences or expresses affection or gladness
behavioral_states.response: Her warmth coexists with an underlying dryness, flatness, or skepticism; emotional expression has a measured, unromantic quality underneath
behavioral_states.observable: Her gladness always had a flat dry floor under it; she tips her head with warmth that registers as dry or measured rather than effusive

behavioral_states.trigger: Vee walks into the room
behavioral_states.response: puts highlighter down immediately, stops reading, redirects full attention to friend
behavioral_states.observable: The highlighter is uncapped and set down; Cassie's gaze shifts from text to Vee; the action signals instant availability and prioritization of friendship over study

behavioral_states.trigger: conversation partner wants to change subject
behavioral_states.response: lets the pivot happen
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie does not resist, redirect, or persist when someone changes conversational direction

behavioral_states.trigger: recognizes a joke or dry line
behavioral_states.response: slightly cocks eyebrow and withholds it for a beat to see if interlocutor recognizes the line before confirming it
behavioral_states.observable: eyebrow cock held for a beat before release

behavioral_states.trigger: Vee enters Cassie's perceptual space
behavioral_states.response: Cassie puts down her highlighter; the gesture signals her full attention has moved to her friend
behavioral_states.observable: The highlighter is lowered / set down; Cassie's focus visibly redirects to Vee

behavioral_states.trigger: Witness physical movement or behavioral detail from other characters (particularly Randi)
behavioral_states.response: Eyes flick to register the detail (location, timing, subtext), then return to neutral; facial expression does not change; no verbal articulation of what was observed
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie's ability to catch and process social/physical detail while maintaining a blank affect; she sees the structure and wrongness but does not call it out

behavioral_states.trigger: returns to chair during conversation or focus
behavioral_states.response: folds into chair with feet up off floor, body compact and still
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie pulls herself into the chair with knees drawn, feet suspended above the floor, creating a contained posture that signals deep listening or processing

behavioral_states.trigger: social invitation or group activity proposal
behavioral_states.response: declines with low-friction excuse formula ('I've got a thing') and redirects others to attend without her
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie uses a characteristic refusal pattern that signals she is not available for group activities, leaving space for others to proceed; the formula is brief, non-accusatory, and implies prior commitment rather than preference-based rejection

behavioral_states.trigger: attending class
behavioral_states.response: unpacks granola bar and retrieves textbooks (wrong one first, then correct one)
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie unpacking a granola bar and the wrong textbook and then the right one in class

behavioral_states.trigger: Vee speaks; Cassie is paying full attention; moment of acknowledgment or acceptance needed
behavioral_states.response: Cassie says 'Okay,' soft, waiting — signals active listening without performance or judgment
behavioral_states.observable: 'Okay,' Cassie said, soft, waiting.

behavioral_states.trigger: sitting, regardless of location or context
behavioral_states.response: pulls feet up (socked feet), typically drawing knees toward chest or holding them with arms
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie sitting with socked feet raised on furniture (bed, chair, etc.) and knees drawn up, often while emotionally engaged or listening intently

behavioral_states.trigger: When Cassie experiences genuine positive feeling toward someone (e.g., her friend Vee)
behavioral_states.response: Expresses gladness flatly, with dry humor; the warmth is present but rendered through dryness rather than conventional warmth cues; she tips her head, the gesture understated
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie's affection reads as dry jokes and flat observations; reader perceives genuine caring precisely because of, not despite, its flat delivery; contrasts sharply with Randi's performed warmth and Vee's more conventional emotional range

behavioral_states.trigger: conversation partner initiates subject change
behavioral_states.response: lets the pivot happen without resistance or attempt to redirect
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie allows conversational shifts to occur; does not push back on topic changes or attempt to steer discussion back to previous subject

behavioral_states.trigger: observes another character's movement or physical positioning in social space
behavioral_states.response: registers the detail with eye movement, then returns to neutral expression; does not vocalize or show emotional reaction
behavioral_states.observable: eyes flick to track movement, face remains unchanged; reports or reacts to perceptual readout without articulating mechanism

behavioral_states.trigger: Vee's entrance or departure from Cassie's immediate attention
behavioral_states.response: Puts highlighter down (focus shifts to Vee); picks it back up (focus returns to task/reading)
behavioral_states.observable: Highlighter capped or uncapped; highlighter in hand or set aside; Cassie's gaze direction shifts between reading material and friend

behavioral_states.trigger: when Vee or another character shares information or seeks Cassie's acknowledgment
behavioral_states.response: says 'Okay' softly, in a waiting register; minimal, non-performative affirmation that signals attentiveness without elaboration
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie's characteristic sign-off or minimal affirmation is 'Okay,' said soft, waiting — reflects her role as listener and her resistance to performed speech

behavioral_states.trigger: Cassie receives good news
behavioral_states.response: Whole face opens; visible gladness; authenticity shows fully
behavioral_states.observable: Face becomes openly glad and unguarded

behavioral_states.trigger: sitting on Vee's bed in shared dorm room space during private moments
behavioral_states.response: pulls sock feet up onto bed, wraps arms around knees, compact folded position
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie sits on edge of Vee's bed with sock feet up and arms around her knees

behavioral_states.trigger: sitting down in any location
behavioral_states.response: pulls feet up (in socks), wraps arms around knees
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie sits with sock feet drawn up and arms wrapped around her knees, a recurring posture visible across multiple scenes and contexts

behavioral_states.trigger: social interaction; being present in shared spaces
behavioral_states.response: contributes minimal verbal content; lets silence and observation dominate her presence
behavioral_states.observable: Cassie produces significantly fewer utterances than peers; her contributions are weighted and deliberate when they occur

behavioral_states.trigger: Vee walks into the room
behavioral_states.response: Cassie puts the highlighter down; her full attention shifts to her friend; she reads Vee instantly
behavioral_states.observable: Highlighter goes down; body language signals immediate focus; perceptual shift from task to person

negations.claim: Cassie was in a sorority / inside that social world
negations.correct_statement: Cassie is unaffiliated — never in a sorority, never inside that social world (same as Vee)

negations.claim: Cassie knows she is Cassandra / the device is named or explained on-page
negations.correct_statement: She does not know she is Cassandra; the device is never named, explained, or commented on by any character including herself
